title: logging to journald with wrong priority
body:
### Issue Description

When containers write to stderr, podman sends it to journald at priority level 3, which is an error.
Altough this might be sensible for some, there are a lot of containers writing a lot of non-errors to stderr.
This in turn makes it very hard to inspect system logs for actual errors, not coming from podman containers.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman run --rm docker.io/alpine sh -c "echo this is an error >&2"`
2. observe journal logs with e.g. `journalctl`


### Describe the results you received

`journalctl`:
![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/24725862/56b50ec6-e08b-4a65-926d-d9981c566998)

### Describe the results you expected

Logging everything at information level probably seems more viable, or an option to deal with this issue some other way.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Fresh install, Ubuntu 22.04.3 LTS

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: What level should it be?  Interested in opening a PR to fix?

> What level should it be? 

Perhaps level 6 (informational)


> Interested in opening a PR to fix?

If that's the right solution for this, sure. But honestly I don't know what would be the ideal solution here.
We can make it configurable but I am very hesitant to change the default.  As mentioned in the issue, the level makes sense for certain use cases and changing that may break them.

Logging is done by conmon so it would need to be changed there and this seems rather unlikely given it is pretty much bug fix only mode. I guess maybe for conmon-rs?

Either way changing the default it is not really an option as podman logs depends on the current prio to know if it is stdout or stderr. Also stdout is already logged at info level so we should not log stderr at same level as podman logs would have no way to seperate the streams.
I am going to close this, it is super unlikely that we change conmon given it maintenance status, maybe we can reconsider this for conmon-rs once the integration is done
